items.headline: Market Closes Lower Amid Tech Sell-off and Geopolitical Tensions on Friday
items.summary: U.S. equities closed lower on Friday, July 18, 2026, as investors rotated out of large-cap growth and technology stocks into value and defensive plays. Renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring, and a significant sell-off in the semiconductor sector contributed to the downturn. Despite encouraging inflation data that eased concerns about Fed rate hikes, overall market sentiment remained cautious heading into the weekend. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Valero Energy Surges on Oil Price Jump
items.summary: Energy stocks led the market, with Valero Energy seeing strong gains as crude oil prices surged over 14% this week due to escalating US-Iran conflict.

items.headline: Diamondback Energy Benefits from Energy Sector Strength
items.summary: As investors rotated into energy, Diamondback Energy recorded notable gains. The sector outperformed amid renewed geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: Apple Rises Despite Tech Weakness
items.summary: Apple ($AAPL) gained 5.8% last week, a notable exception amidst broader weakness in the 'Mag 7' and the tech sector, positioning itself near the top U.S. market cap spot.

items.headline: IBM Plunges After Weak Earnings Report
items.summary: International Business Machines ($IBM) stock plummeted over 20% in a single day after releasing weaker-than-expected earnings, citing customers prioritizing hardware spending.

items.headline: Netflix Shares Sink on Disappointing Guidance
items.summary: Netflix ($NFLX) shares dropped more than 7% on Friday after its third-quarter revenue guidance fell below Wall Street's expectations.

items.headline: Broadcom Hit as AI Trade Suffers
items.summary: Broadcom ($AVGO) was down 7.3% last week as investors rotated away from technology and momentum stocks amid concerns about AI infrastructure valuations.

items.headline: Broad Sector Rotation Continues: Tech Out, Value In
items.summary: The market witnessed a continued rotation on Friday, with investors shifting away from high-flying technology and growth stocks, particularly those in the AI infrastructure and semiconductor space, towards value-oriented sectors like Energy, Financials, Healthcare, and Consumer Staples. This move reflects a desire for stability and a reassessment of tech valuations.

items.headline: Inflation Cools, Easing Fed Rate Hike Concerns
items.summary: Encouraging economic data released this week showed a moderation in consumer (CPI) and producer (PPI) inflation for June, leading to a drop in the odds of a Federal Reserve rate hike this year. However, inflation remains above the Fed's 2.0% target, with volatile energy prices posing an ongoing risk.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Kicks Off Strong, Banks Lead
items.summary: The second-quarter 2026 earnings season is off to a strong start, with many companies reporting solid results and major American banks showing significant profit jumps, partly due to strong investment banking divisions. The S&P 500 operating EPS growth is estimated around 22.9-24.7% year-over-year.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices to New Highs
items.summary: Escalating conflict between the U.S. and Iran, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, caused crude oil prices to surge by over 14% this week. This ongoing geopolitical instability continues to be a significant factor influencing market sentiment and inflation expectations.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Improves, But Risks Remain
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment rose in July to a five-month high. However, this improvement largely reflects conditions prior to the renewed Middle East conflict, and the upward momentum may be difficult to sustain if rising gasoline prices persist.
